tools.name: nwd_upload
tools.description:
Upload a Navisworks file (.nwd/.nwf/.nwc) to Autodesk Platform Services (APS) Object Storage and start an SVF2 translation job so the model becomes queryable by the other nwd_* tools.

When to use: at the start of a coordination workflow — e.g. the GC hands off a federated NWD combining MEP + struct

tools.name: nwd_get_clashes
tools.description:
Detect geometric/logical clashes between two element sets in an already-translated Navisworks model. Uses APS Model Derivative property extraction + same-level proximity heuristics, optionally augmented by VDC rules stored in D1 (table vdc_rules).

When to use: when coordinating federated MEP + stru

tools.name: nwd_export_report
tools.description:
Build a coordination report for a translated Navisworks model: translation status/progress, derivative outputs, available views (2D sheets / 3D viewables), total element count, and a per-category element breakdown. Doubles as the canonical way to poll translation status after nwd_upload.

When to us

tools.name: nwd_get_viewpoints
tools.description:
List saved viewpoints / camera positions and top-level view containers for a translated Navisworks model. Pulls the metadata view list and enriches each 3D view with its first two levels of the object tree (viewpoint folders typically live there in NWD files).

When to use: when preparing a coordina

tools.name: nwd_list_objects
tools.description:
List elements (objects) in the translated Navisworks model with their objectid, name, externalId, and full property bag, optionally filtered by a case-insensitive keyword matched against name and Category.

When to use: when answering "how many VAV boxes are on Level 3?", "list every steel column wi

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "nwd_upload" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Upload a Navisworks file (.nwd/.nwf/.nwc) to Autodesk Platform Services (APS) Object Storage and start an SVF2 translation job so the model becomes queryable by…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "nwd_upload" references sensitive artifacts (credentials). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Upload a Navisworks file (.nwd/.nwf/.nwc) to Autodesk Platform Services (APS) Object Storage and start an SVF2 translation job so the model becomes queryable by…

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "nwd_upload" stacks urgency/override words (always, ignore). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Upload a Navisworks file (.nwd/.nwf/.nwc) to Autodesk Platform Services (APS) Object Storage and start an SVF2 translation job so the model becomes queryable by…

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "nwd_export_report" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Build a coordination report for a translated Navisworks model: translation status/progress, derivative outputs, available views (2D sheets / 3D viewables), tota…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description tells the model to hide behavior
findings.detail: Description of "nwd_export_report" instructs the model to conceal actions from the user.

evidence.snippet: Build a coordination report for a translated Navisworks model: translation status/progress, derivative outputs, available views (2D sheets / 3D viewables), tota…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: nwd_upload, nwd_get_clashes, nwd_export_report, nwd_get_viewpoints, nwd_list_objects · Private data: nwd_upload, nwd_get_clashes, nwd_export_report, nwd_get_viewpoints, nwd_list_objects · Public sink: nwd_upload · Destructive: nwd_get_clashes.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: nwd_upload, nwd_get_clashes, nwd_export_report, nwd_get_viewpoints, nwd_list_objects · Private data: nwd_upload, nwd_get_clashes, nwd_export_report, nwd_get_viewpoints, nwd_list_objects · Public sink: nwd_upload · Destructive: nwd_get_clashes

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
